Quality Engineering

Credit Hours:  3

Catalog Description

Quality Engineering. (2-3). Credit 3. Strategic approach to implementing quality,

process and business improvement methods using data analysis tools; total quality

management and six sigma approaches to define, measure, analyze, improve and

control processes; principles of lean engineering; control charts; process capability

analysis; basic metrology, applied statistics, lean principles and process capability.

Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ISEN 310 and ISEN 230.

Additional Course Details

ISEN 350 will follow a flipped classroom format. Classes will meet on

Wednesdays, and next week's module will be posted at 1 PM. Weekly

modules begin with an overview and include lecture videos, a reading

assignment, a problem set, and a module quiz. Most lecture videos are brief

—approximately 20 minutes—and are designed to introduce key concepts.

While they are not comprehensive, they will serve as a foundation for each

module and will guide your study. Further amplification of the material will be

found in the textbook, reference materials, and classroom discussions.

Success in this course requires active engagement. You are fully responsible

for your own learning and performance. I am here to support and guide you,

but you must take initiative.

Wednesday class meetings are for you and will be used to answer questions,

review critical concepts, work through examples, develop problem-solving

skills, and discuss problems that are challenging you in \ current problem set.

These sessions are essential to your understanding of the material and

performing well in this class.

Lab Attendance is Mandatory to receive credit for a lab. Missing two

labs will result in an F grade in the course.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in ISEN 310 and ISEN 230.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Apply Six Sigma methodologies to improve process quality

Identify waste and reduce non-value-added activities in processes

Apply statistical concepts and data analytic methods to reduce product

and service system variability

Think  critically, develop computer codes, and write technical reports

Implement the methods and tools of Lean-Six Sigma in engineering

practice

Write professional and informative technical reports

Special Course Designation

UWRT - University Required Writing Intensive

ISEN 350 is now certified as a Writing (W) course. In ISEN 350, you’ll put

Quality Engineering methods into practice through hands-on lab activities and

document your findings in professional technical reports. Because this is a

Writing (W) course, you’ll also develop strong written communication skills

that are highly valued by employers. Faculty from the ISEN Writing and

Student Success Center will be available to guide and support you in

becoming a more confident and effective technical communicator.

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Required

Introduction to Statistical Quality Control

Authors:  Douglas C. Montgomrty

Publisher:  Wiley

Edition:  Eighth

Notes:

is required. Be sure to  bring your calculator to all Lectures & Labs .

Software:  MATLAB ,  Python  (equivalent programming language),  EXCEL , and

Minitab. (Minitab instructions provided in Lab)

Course Materials & Copyright: All documents used in this course are

copyrighted. The word "documents" means all materials generated for this

class, including, but not limited to, syllabi,   quizzes, exams, problem sets, slide

sets, videos, and all materials appearing on the class website or sent to you

via email. Because these materials are copyrighted, you do not have the right

to copy or share any of them for any purpose other than your personal

academic use unless I expressly grant permission

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

The following late penalties apply:

1 day late: 25% deduction

2 days late: 50% deduction

3 or more days late: Grade of zero

Plan ahead to avoid penalties.

Module Quizzes must be submitted on-time for credit.

Examination Schedule

Test 1: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 (Lecture Period)

Test 2: (Final Exam): Tuesday,  May 5, 2026: 10:30 AM – 12:30 PM

Following Department Policy,  I will request the  Aggie Honor Court  to assign

a grade of F* for any breach of the Aggie Honor Code, no matter how minimal

the offense may be.

Because writing, analysis, and critical thinking are core learning outcomes of

this course:

All written work, computer code, and problem-solving assignments must

be your own work and completed individually

Collaboration of any kind is not permitted

Course Specific Attendance Policy

Lab attendance is mandatory to receive credit for the lab assignment. Missing

two lab assignments will result in a final course grade of F.
